title: Preparing Microbiome Samples for Cryo Shipment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d4mth","text":"This is a generic protocol written for any collaborators or contributing institutions sending samples to the Rich Lab at Otterbein University for microbiome or other genetic analyses. The protocol is written for samples that have already been frozen. They may be stored in any stabilizing solution or dried.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cm8ve","text":"Use proper aseptic technique during this procedure. Do not open sample vials, and make sure you are wearing lab gloves the entire time. Also consider wearing cryo-gloves of some kind to protect your hands from cold burns.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7o2m5","text":"Make sure all samples are stored in the smallest, most secure and clearly-labeled vessels possible. This will keep the shipment cheaper, safer, and ensure that all samples remain frozen. To ensure that this is the case, I am including an optional stage at the beginning of this protocol for transferring samples to smaller tubes and boxes before preparing the shipment. If you carry out this procedure, please be extra careful about aseptic technique. If possible, carry out these steps inside an operational biological safety cabinet or some other enclosure with a HEPA filtration system. Do not let any tools cross-contaminate DNA/RNA between samples. If you need to reuse a tool for two samples, wash it using a 10% bleach solution. Remember that ethanol will not sterilize genetic material.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7lim","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2t8nk","text":"If samples are already in the proper storage containers for shipment, be sure that you have made arrangements for the time and date of pickup with Dr. Rich or with Fedex. Pre-cool all of the materials, including the box, in a   freezer (or the coldest option available), and do not add the dry ice and close the box until just before shipment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":226,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"5B9D5FD0BCCD11EBB6D517A4F5753EED","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"-80","unit":10}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4oift","text":"If you open any of the tubes then treat them as biohazards. Wear proper PPE at all times. That includes proper gloves to protect you from ultra low temps.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"4d9nc","text":"If you are transferring samples to new tubes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"demp7","text":"Sterile, disposable tweezers","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7t6mi","text":"10% bleach solution and kimwipes for cleaning and sterilizing","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7459","text":"Biological Safety Cabinet or some secure area for dealing with biological material","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"90asj","text":"Sterile, RNase/DNase free tubes (1.5 - 2 mL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4tg97","text":"Biohazard disposal bag","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"db798","text":"PPE: gloves, lab coat, optional face mask","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ae56","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44pk4","text":"For shipment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2897a","text":"Freezer boxes with lids (can be cardboard or other cryo-safe material)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fflr0","text":"Styrofoam cooler with lid","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1q0tq","text":"Dry ice","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"epe7m","text":"Sturdy cardboard box","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"79ftb","text":"Fedex Hazardous materials Labels","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6nmsu","text":"Fedex shipment labels","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1roat","text":"PPE: lab gloves, cryo-gloves, lab coat","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: On ice Keep all samples on cooling blocks or ice during these procedures.

Label sterile, RNase/DNase-free tubes for each sample that you will be transferring. You should label them using a thin-tip permanent marker, and copy the information on the previous tube. If that information is not visible, add your initials and the date. Try to make out the sample ID # and add a ? if necessary. Make sure there are labels on the side and the top of each tube. This ensures that the sample will still be labeled if we lose the cap and that you will be able to easily see the sample number on the tops of tubes looking down into the storage box. Be careful not to touch the inside of the lids or the tubes, even with your gloved hands. Also try not to touch your face or hair while wearing the gloves, and don't cough or lean over the open tubes. If you need to touch something that might contaminate your gloves, then change out a new pair.

Use the sterile tweezers to carefully transfer all of the material from an old tube into the new, fully labeled tube. Again, take care not to touch your finger to the sample or the inside of the lid. Do not use the same tweezers for more than one sample, or if you must, then clean the tweezers between samples with a 10% bleach solution.

As soon as you complete one sample, cap both tubes. Put the old tube into a biohazard disposal bag. Put the new tube back into the freezer immediately or keep it on ice. The most important thing is that you do not transfer genetic material from one sample to the next when you do this. Consider rubbing a few drops of the 10% bleach solution on your gloves between samples or change gloves if you think you inadvertently touched any sample material before you move to the next one.

After you finish all of the tubes, carefully arrange them in the sample box that I provided and double check that all of the caps are labeled with the sample #. Put this box into the freezer until shipment.

Properly dispose of the biohazard bag with the old tubes. This will depend on your lab's protocols, but you may need to put autoclave tape on the bag, autoclave it for sterilization, and then put it in the trash for disposal. Only materials that may have remaining biological materials on them are considered biohazard waste requiring autoclaving before disposal.
Proceed to the steps for sample shipment.

Make sure all tubes are securely capped and labeled. Tubes must be placed in a secondary container. For small tubes (2 mL and less), this should be freezer boxes. For large tubes, this can be sealed ziploc bags.

Line the inside of a styrofoam cooler with a large, plastic bag. Put this inside a freezer ( -80 °C if available) overnight to pre-chill it. Overnight Do not add the dry ice yet. Just add the plastic liner as shown here.

Put these boxes or bags inside the plastic-lined styrofoam cooler. Make sure you have left enough room for dry ice around the edges and on top. Keep this inside the freezer until immediately before shipment.

Make sure all labels are correctly placed on the outer cardboard box. Proper shipping name: " DRY ICE " UN NUMBER: " UN 1845 " Class 9 Hazard Label (attached) Dry_Ice_Label.pdf Net quantity of dry ice " 2 kg" FedEx Express electronic shipping label with shipper and recipient markings on it: Your name and address as shipper Recipient name and address: Rich Lab c/o Erin Ulrich Department of Biology and Earth Science Otterbein University 1 South Grove Street Westerville, OH 43081

Assemble the documents that will need to go inside the cardboard box (but outside the styrofoam cooler). That includes the shipment letter from Dr. Rich and the sample manifest (both will be provided).

I will schedule a pickup for the package. Soon before, you should place the styrofoam box inside the cardboard box. Close the plastic liner as shown below. Surround it with dry ice and place the styrofoam lid on top. Tape the box as shown in step 5. Do not seal the lid on. Air must be able to escape the box as the dry ice evaporates.
Hand off the package for pickup! Thank you!
creator: Alicia M Rich
